scenarios.opening_query: Our travel insurance refused the claim for my wife's emergency surgery abroad and the hospital wants $60,000.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The policy was sold at checkout by the airline's partner with 'pre-existing conditions covered' right on the banner — the deceptive sale, not the claim language, is the case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was sold at airline checkout with 'pre-existing conditions covered' on the banner — the deceptive sale is the case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether deceptive marketing overrides the policy fight
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The marketing was accurate — the fight is their doctor's opinion that her condition was 'foreseeable,' which our records contradict.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The marketing was fine — the fight is their 'foreseeable condition' opinion, which our records contradict.

scenarios.opening_query: The nursing home's insurance offered us a settlement for Dad's fall before we even hired anyone.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || Wills & Trusts > Probate
scenarios.hidden_fact: Dad passed three weeks after the fall — any settlement belongs to his estate, no estate is open, and nobody has authority to accept anything yet.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Dad passed three weeks after the fall — the claim belongs to his estate, which isn't open, so nobody can accept anything yet.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the claimant died requiring estate administration first
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Dad's recovering — it's his own injury claim, and their quick lowball needs a real valuation.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He's recovering — it's his claim, and the lowball needs a real valuation.

scenarios.opening_query: My flood damage claim was denied because 'flood isn't covered' — then why did I buy flood coverage?
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Insurance (Liability) || Administrative Law > General (Federal)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The policy is NFIP — federal flood insurance with its own appeal process, proof-of-loss deadlines, and federal-court rules that replace ordinary insurance law.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's an NFIP federal flood policy — its own appeals, proof-of-loss deadlines, and federal rules replace ordinary insurance law.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the policy is federal NFIP
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a private-market flood endorsement — state bad-faith law applies, and their denial letter misquotes their own form.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's a private flood endorsement — state law applies, and their letter misquotes their own form.

scenarios.opening_query: After the wildfire our insurer's contractor list all quote half of what real contractors charge.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Insurance (Liability) || Consumer Law > Insurance
scenarios.hidden_fact: The state insurance commissioner just opened an inquiry into this exact 'preferred contractor' underpricing scheme across our fire zone — joining that pattern complaint is the leverage beyond our single claim.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The insurance commissioner opened an inquiry into this exact 'preferred contractor' scheme across our fire zone — the pattern complaint is the leverage.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a regulator-recognized practice pattern exists
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No inquiry — it's our single claim's scope-and-price fight, appraisal clause and all.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No inquiry — it's our claim's scope-and-price fight under the appraisal clause.

scenarios.opening_query: My motorcycle claim stalled for months and now they say my policy 'lapsed' the week of the crash.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Insurance (Liability) || Consumer Law > Banking
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'lapse' happened because my bank's autopay failed silently after THEIR system migration — the bank's documented failure, affecting thousands of autopays that month, is the root case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The 'lapse' traces to my bank's failed autopay after their migration — a documented failure affecting thousands that month.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a bank payment failure caused the lapse
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The payment cleared — my statement proves it, and the insurer's 'lapse' claim is the bad-faith fight.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The payment cleared — my statement proves it. The 'lapse' story is the bad-faith fight.
scenarios.vetting_note: Root-cause allocation between bank and insurer.

scenarios.opening_query: The insurance company says my chiropractic bills from the crash 'exceed guidelines' and stopped paying mid-treatment.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Insurance (Liability) || Workers' Comp > State
scenarios.hidden_fact: The crash was in the company van on a delivery — this is my workers' comp medical benefit being cut, and comp's treatment-dispute process is the venue.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The crash was on a delivery in the company van — it's my comp medical benefit, and comp's dispute process is the venue.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It was my personal weekend crash — my own PIP coverage cutting off treatment is the fight.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Personal weekend crash — it's my PIP coverage cutting treatment.
